                                     %target     = VALUE #( ( ErrorCode          = wa_errorcode_crt-error_code
                                                              %control-ErrorCode = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ).
      APPEND ls_errorcode_create TO lt_errorcode_create.

    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT it_errorcodestext_crt INTO DATA(wa_errorcodestext_crt).

      ls_errorcodestext_create = VALUE #(
          %tky-ErrorCode = wa_errorcodestext_crt-error_code
          %target        = VALUE #( ( Langu                = wa_errorcodestext_crt-langu
                                      %control-Langu       = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                      Description          = wa_errorcodestext_crt-description
                                      %control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ).

      APPEND ls_errorcodestext_create TO lt_errorcodestext_create.

    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = TransportRequest ) )

           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           CREATE BY \_ErrorCode FROM lt_errorcode_create

           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_crt)
           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_crt)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_crt).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode
           CREATE BY \_ErrorCodeText FROM lt_errorcodestext_create

           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodestext_crt)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodestext_crt)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodestext_crt).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_create)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_create).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.


    IF failed_create IS INITIAL.
      LOOP AT m_errorcodes_crt-errorcode INTO DATA(wa_errorcodes_message).
        APPEND wa_errorcodes_message-ErrorCode TO lt_errorcode_crt_message.
      ENDLOOP.

      lv_string_errorcode_crt = concat_lines_of( table = lt_errorcode_crt_message
                                                 sep   = `, ` ).

      MESSAGE ID 'ZCL_ERRORCODES_MSG' TYPE 'S' NUMBER 001 WITH lv_string_errorcode_crt INTO DATA(lv_message) ##NEEDED.
      TRY.
          ia_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
          cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
        CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_pass).
          DATA(lv_bali_runtime_pass) = lx_bali_runtime_pass->get_text( ).
      ENDTRY.
    ELSE.

      LOOP AT reported_create-%other INTO DATA(ls_message).
        DATA(ls_text) = ls_message->if_message~get_longtext( ).
      ENDLOOP.

      IF ls_text IS NOT INITIAL.

        TRY.
            DATA(lv_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                                   text     = | { ls_text } | ).

            ia_log->add_item( item = lv_free_text ).

            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                       assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

          CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
            DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_errorocodes.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           FAILED DATA(f_delete_tr)
           MAPPED DATA(m_delete_tr)
           REPORTED DATA(r_delete_tr).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           CREATE FIELDS ( TransportRequestID ) WITH VALUE #( ( TransportRequestID  = transportrequest ) )
           DELETE FROM VALUE #( ( SingletonID = 1 ) )

           FAILED DATA(f_delete)
           MAPPED DATA(m_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(r_delete).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_delete).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.


    IF failed_delete IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE ID 'ZCL_ERRORCODES_MSG' TYPE 'S' NUMBER 003 INTO DATA(lv_message_delete) ##NEEDED.
      TRY.
          ia_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
          cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
        CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_pass).
          DATA(lv_bali_runtime_pass) = lx_bali_runtime_pass->get_text( ).
      ENDTRY.

    ELSE.
      LOOP AT reported_delete-%other INTO DATA(ls_message).
        DATA(ls_text) = ls_message->if_message~get_longtext( ).
      ENDLOOP.

      IF ls_text IS NOT INITIAL.

        TRY.
            DATA(lv_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                                   text     = | { ls_text } | ).

            ia_log->add_item( item = lv_free_text ).

            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                       assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

          CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
            DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_errorocodes.
    DATA lt_errorcodestext_update_i TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCodeText.
    DATA lt_errorcodes_upd_message  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF string.
    DATA lv_string_errorcodes_upd   TYPE String.

    IF it_errorcodes_update IS NOT INITIAL OR it_errorcodestext_update IS NOT INITIAL.
      LOOP AT it_errorcodestext_update INTO DATA(ls_errorcodetext_update).
        ls_errorcodetext_update-%control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
        APPEND ls_errorcodetext_update TO lt_errorcodestext_update_i.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( transportrequestid )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonID        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           ENTITY ErrorCodeText
           UPDATE FROM lt_errorcodestext_update_i

           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_upd)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_upd)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_upd).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S

           FAILED DATA(failed_update)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_update).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.

    IF f_errorcodes_upd IS INITIAL.

      LOOP AT lt_errorcodestext_update_i INTO DATA(wa_errorcodestext_upd_message).
        APPEND wa_errorcodestext_upd_message-ErrorCode TO lt_errorcodes_upd_message.
      ENDLOOP.

      lv_string_errorcodes_upd = concat_lines_of( table = lt_errorcodes_upd_message
                                                  sep   = `, ` ).

      MESSAGE ID 'ZCL_ERRORCODES_MSG' TYPE 'S' NUMBER 002 WITH lv_string_errorcodes_upd INTO DATA(lv_message) ##NEEDED.
      TRY.
          ia_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
          cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
        CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_pass).
          DATA(lv_bali_runtime_pass) = lx_bali_runtime_pass->get_text( ).
      ENDTRY.

    ELSE.

      LOOP AT reported_update-%other INTO DATA(ls_message).
        DATA(ls_text) = ls_message->if_message~get_longtext( ).
      ENDLOOP.

      IF ls_text IS NOT INITIAL.

        TRY.
            DATA(lv_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                                   text     = | { ls_text } | ).

            ia_log->add_item( item = lv_free_text ).

            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                       assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

          CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
            DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_tables_create.
    iat_errorcodes_crt = it_errorcodes_crt.
    iat_errorcodestext_crt = it_errorcodestext_crt.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_tables_update.
    iat_errorcodes_update = it_errorcodes_update.
    iat_errorcodestext_update = it_errorcodestext_update.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodes_for_create.
    et_errorcodes_crt = iat_errorcodes_crt.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodestext_for_create.
    et_errorcodestext_crt = iat_errorcodestext_crt.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodestext_for_update.
    et_errorcodestext_update = iat_errorcodestext_update.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodes_for_update.
    et_errorcodes_update = iat_errorcodes_update.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Test your Development

Prerequisite

To run the application job, users must have the authorization provided by a Business Catalog. The Business Catalog must have two "Business Catalog IAM App Assignments". One for the Application Job and one for the Business Configuration Maintenance App: 

Additionally, to view the customizing entries in the Custom Business Configurations App, users also need the SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT business role.

Run the Application Job

Open the Application Job App and select "Create" to create a new Application Job:

Choose the Application Job Template:

Maintain the scheduling options and provide a valid transport request. Schedule the Application Job by clicking "Schedule": 

Check the Application Job Log to determine if the error codes haven been inserted and updated correctly:

Users with the assigned business role SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT can now check the Custom Business Configurations App whether the customizing has been inserted and updated correctly:

If incorrect error codes are provided, an error message will show up in the Application Job Log:

Summary

This blog post explains how a SaaS provider can deliver customizing for a SaaS solution by using a generated Business Configuration Maintenance Object. It also covers the use of an application job, utilizing the Entity Manipulation Language to create, update, and delete customizing entries.

entry.title: SAP Developer News February 27th, 2025
entry.content:
PODCAST: 

https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/02/27/sap-developer-news-february-27th-2025/

DESCRIPTION 

New Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities videos!!! 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-power-of-joule-for-developers-abap-ai-capabilities/ba-p/14019716?source=social-Developer_Community-Upskill-Video-Educate%2FEnable-Global-N%2FA-SAP.com&campaigncode=CRM-YA23-SMS-1941768&sprinklrid=16176097274 
  • https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAt9lvPw0gF4E3nwbJD0EUe 

 SAP Integration Suite Onboarding Webinar 

  • Event link: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-events/sap-integration-suite-onboarding-webinar-global/ev-p/14024129 
  • Registration Page: https://www.sap.com/events/2025-03-18-online-sap-integration-suite-onboarding-webinar.html 

 SAP Inside Track Bangalore - This Saturday 

  • Registration Page: https://konfhub.com/sap-inside-track-bengaluru-march-2025-business-ai-edition 
  • Event link: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-1st-march-2025/ec-p/13990380 

 How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-build-a-responsive-ui-for-a-rap-business-object-that-starts-an/ba-p/14027487 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 New Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities videos!!! 

1:14 SAP Integration Suite Onboarding Webinar  

2:07 SAP Inside Track Bangalore - This Saturday 

3:04 How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP developer News for February 27th, 2025.

[Rich] Hey friends, Rich Heilman here. Last week, I told you about the release of Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities, and I just wanted it to make you aware of some two-minute of ABAP cloud videos that we are publishing over the next few weeks. These videos are specifically around the Joule for developer ABAP AI capabilities and should give you a quick overview of what it is and how to use it and some tips and tricks as well. I've created a special playlist on the developer's YouTube channel for your convenience, and I'll post a link in the comments as well. The first few are out there now with a fun little introduction, and then we get right into the good stuff in the next episodes of the series, introducing you to Joule and how to get started using it today. These demos are coming straight out of our development organization by our developers for our developers. So check them out today. Bye for now.

[Ajay] Hello, everyone. If you are interested in SAP Integration Suite or curious about what it is, we have got a great new webinar coming up that I would like to tell you about. You can join the global webinar on SAP Integration Suite onboarding on March 18th, 2025 from 3 to 4PM CET. This session is geared towards all personas looking to get started with SAP Integration Suite to help drive your digital transformation. You will get an overview of all the available SAP Integration Suite capabilities and learn how to activate them. The session covers frequently asked questions, fundamentals and beginner tips to help you get started with SAP Integration Suite. We look forward to seeing you at this event. The registration link for this webinar is available in the description.

[Mamikee] This Saturday, March 1st, 2025, SAP Inside Track, Bengaluru is bringing together developers, experts, and innovators for a full day of learning, networking, and hands-on AI experiences live and in-person. This is your chance to explore cutting-edge AI solutions across SAP BTP, S/4 HANA, Ariba, concur, and Success Factor. You'll hear from directly from top SAP experts about the future of Enterprise AI and discover real-run success stories from businesses already leveraging AI to drive innovation. You'll get handled experience in interactive workshops, explore live demos, and see how AI seamlessly integrates into SAP solutions. This is your opportunity to connect with SAP professionals, developers, and AI inclusives all in one place. And remember, this is a in-person-only event, so don't miss out. You can secure your spot in the link below.

[Rich] Wait, wait, wait, wait. I'm still Rich Heilman, and I forgot to tell you about a new blog post from our good friend Andre Fischer. And in his blog post, Andre shows us how to build a responsive user interface for RAP business objects that start as an asynchronous process. So in this blog post, Andre shows us an easy way to start that process using the background process framework and shows us an easy way to update the user interface with the result thanks to a new feature which has recently been released which allows to refresh the user interface of an SAP Fiori Elements application by simply raising an event. So very cool stuff here. Make sure to check out Andre's blog post today and I'll link to it in the description and bye for now. And I'm really going to go this time. Bye-bye. Thank you.

entry.title: Integration of QR Code Scanning in SAP Module Pool Using HTML Service
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog provides an extensive overview of the steps required to implement a Scan QR functionality in a module pool program by triggering an HTML QR service with a pushbutton and returning the retrieved data to the screen after scanning, ensuring seamless integration between the SAP module pool program and the HTML QR scanning service to enable efficient QR code processing.

Steps:

Step 1: Create a class using transaction se24 and specify IF_HTTP_EXTENSION in the interface section. This class is intended to handle HTTPS requests and responses in the context of web-based applications or services, particularly custom handlers in the Internet Communication Framework.

Step 2: Declare IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST in the methods section by keeping it in public visibility.

Step 3: Click on the method and go to source code to deploy the HTML QR scan code in it.

Step 4: To retrieve the HTTP request method of an incoming request in SAP's ICF (Internet Communication Framework) service, attach the below available HTML code for scan QR functionality.

 

 

 

METHOD IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST.
DATA: lv_request_method TYPE string,
      lv_html           TYPE string,
      lt_html_table     TYPE TABLE OF string,
      gv_json           TYPE char250, "string,
      gs_session        TYPE zpp_tab_sess_id,
      lv_var1           TYPE string,
      lv_var2           TYPE string,
      lv_var3           TYPE string,
      lv_var4           TYPE string,
      lv_var5           TYPE string,
      lv_var6           TYPE string,
      lv_var7           TYPE string,
      lv_var8           TYPE string,
      lv_var9           TYPE string.
      lv_request_method = server->request->get_method( ).
  CASE lv_request_method.
      WHEN 'GET'.
        CLEAR : gv_json.
        lt_html_table = VALUE #(
        ( CONV string( '<!DOCTYPE html>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<html lang="en">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<head>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<meta charset="UTF-8">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<title>QR Code Scanner</title>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<style>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    .message-container {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; margin-top: 20px;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    #statusMessage {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        text-align: center; font-size: 15px; margin-bottom: 5px;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    #rescanButton {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        display: none; margin: 0 auto; padding: 5px 10px; font-size: 10px; cursor: pointer;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</style>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</head>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<body>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<div class="container">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    <h1>Scan QR Codes</h1>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    <div class="section">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        <div style="width: 500px" id="reader" name="QRCodeData"></div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        <div class="message-container">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            <p id="statusMessage"></p>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            <button id="rescanButton" onclick="rescan()">Rescan</button>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        </div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    </div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<script src="https://unpkg.com/html5-qrcode"></script>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<script>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    function onScanSuccess(decodedText, decodedResult) {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const barcodetxt = decodedText;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        console.log(decodedText);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const sessionID = urlParams.get("SESSION_ID") || '''';' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const port = window.location.port;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const senddata = async (barcodetxt, sessionID, port) => {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            try {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                const res = await fetch(`https://<server>:${port}/sap/bc/zsrv_evp_qrscan`, {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    method: "POST",' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    headers: {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                        "Content-Type": "application/json"' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    },' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    body: JSON.stringify({ barcodetxt, sessionID })' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                });' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                if (res.ok) {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    document.getElementById("statusMessage").innerText = "Updated Successfully!";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    document.getElementById("statusMessage").style.color = "green";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    setTimeout(() => {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                        window.open("", "_self", "");' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                        window.close();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    }, 2000);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                } else {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    showErrorMessage();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            } catch (error) {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                showErrorMessage();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                console.error("Error updating data:", error);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        };' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        senddata(barcodetxt, sessionID, port);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        html5QrcodeScanner.clear();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    function showErrorMessage() {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const statusMessage = document.getElementById("statusMessage");' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        statusMessage.innerText = "Request failed. Please try again.";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        statusMessage.style.color = "red";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const rescanButton = document.getElementById("rescanButton");' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        rescanButton.style.display = "inline-block";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    function rescan() {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        document.getElementById("statusMessage").innerText = "";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        document.getElementById("rescanButton").style.display = "none";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        html5QrcodeScanner.render(onScanSuccess);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    var html5QrcodeScanner = new Html5QrcodeScanner(' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        "reader", { fps: 10, qrbox: 250 });' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    html5QrcodeScanner.render(onScanSuccess);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</script>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</body>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</html>' ) )
        ).

        CONCATENATE LINES OF lt_html_table INTO lv_html SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>cr_lf.
        server->response->set_cdata( lv_html ).
        server->response->set_status( code = 200 reason = 'OK' ).

      WHEN 'POST'.
        server->request->get_cdata( RECEIVING data = gv_json ).
        lv_html = gv_json.
        server->response->set_content_type( 'text/html' ).
        server->response->set_cdata( lv_html ).
        SPLIT gv_json AT '"' INTO lv_var1  lv_var2 lv_var3 lv_var4 lv_var5 
                                  lv_var6 lv_var7 lv_var8 lv_var9.
        IF lv_var4 IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_var8 IS NOT INITIAL.
          gs_session = VALUE #( session_id = lv_var8
                                qr_value   = lv_var4
                                created_on = sy-datum
                                created_at = sy-uzeit
                                created_by = sy-uname ).
        IF gs_session IS NOT INITIAL.
          MODIFY  zpp_tab_sess_id FROM gs_session.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
        ELSE.
           ROLLBACK WORK.
        ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
        CLEAR : gv_json,gs_session.
    ENDCASE.
ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

Step 5: After building the logic in se24, bind the class in SICF transaction by giving hierarchy name as SERVICE.

Step 6:  Create a service name in the below navigated hierarchy.

Step 7: Assign the class in which the HTML code has been embedded in the handler list of the created service by double clicking on the service.

Step 8: Create a z-table to store the session id and its data while scanning the QR to avoid the data mismatch.

Step 7: Create a pushbutton in your required screen by using module pool program and name it as Scan QR by keeping its function code as ‘SCAN’

Step 9: In the PAI part write the logic to scan the QR

Code Explanation:

9.1: cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_c32_static( RECEIVING uuid = gv_uuid ) will be used to create GUID’s for the new session while triggering the scan pushbutton.

9.2: gc_srv refers to the service path which is created in SICF t-code along with the session ID .

9.3: gc_host is the HTTPS host link lv_post is the port for the particular server and gv_uuid is the created guid for each session.

 

 

 

gc_srv  TYPE string   VALUE '/sap/bc/zsrv_evp_qrscan?SESSION_ID=',
gv_qr         TYPE char50,
gv_uuid       TYPE guid_32.
gv_url        TYPE char200,

 

 

 

 

 

 

WHEN 'SCAN'.
      CLEAR : gv_qr, gv_uuid.
*****************To get GUID for each new session***********************
      cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_c32_static( RECEIVING uuid = gv_uuid ).
****************Changing the URL according to development and production client*******************
      zcl_ril_constant=>fetch_constant_data(
  EXPORTING
    iv_program_name = 'EVP_PORT'
    iv_field        = 'PORT'
    iv_active_flag  = ''
  IMPORTING
    et_table        = DATA(lt_const)
       ).
      DATA(lv_port) = VALUE #( lt_const[ field_name = 'PORT']-zlow OPTIONAL ).

      CONCATENATE gc_host lv_port gc_srv gv_uuid INTO gv_url.
********************Call Browser triggers the browser for QR scan*********************************
      CALL FUNCTION 'CALL_BROWSER'
        EXPORTING
          url                    = gv_url
          window_name            = 'NEW'
        EXCEPTIONS
          frontend_not_supported = 1
          frontend_error         = 2
          prog_not_found         = 3
          no_batch               = 4
          unspecified_error      = 5
          OTHERS                 = 6.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        CLEAR : gv_url.
        MESSAGE text-069 TYPE 'I'.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE text-072 TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
*******************Mapping the GUID to the Scan QR field*******************************
      SELECT * FROM zpp_tab_sess_id
      INTO TABLE (lt_id)
      WHERE session_id = _uuid.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        DATA(lv_qr_data) = VALUE #( lt_id[ session_id = gv_uuid ]-qr_value OPTIONAL ).
        IF lv_qr_data IS NOT INITIAL .
          gv_qr = lv_qr_data.
        ENDIF.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE text-072 TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      ENDIF.

 

 

 

Conclusion:

Incorporating QR Code Scanning in a SAP Module Pool with HTML Service offers easy data capture and real-time processing within the SAP GUI. Users can efficiently scan and process QR codes by embedding an HTML page in a Custom Container, using SAP SICF services, and storing scanned data through ABAP methods. This method improves user experience, eliminates human data entry, and enhances system automation.

entry.title: Unable to Read Repository at / Error Connecting to URL Error In Eclipse IDE-SAP BTP / S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Everyone , 

For the past few months / weeks I have been getting either of these two errors  

1. Error connecting to https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ . 

2. unable to read repository  https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest or URL https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/<eclipse-version> . 

while installing the ADT Tools in the eclipse . 

I have extensively seen this error in the 2024-12 version of the IDE. 

there are many solutions being discussed around but I have found a rather simple solution , which I would like to share .

Steps : 

1. Go to https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ for getting the ADT tools repository for your eclipse version .

2. select the repository  Link which you will be using to install the eclipse , in my case it will be https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/2024-12 .

3. Open the link in a new tab.

4. Save the Certificate for this site ; I'm using the Google Chrome browser , I will mention the steps for Microsoft Edge Below in this Blog .

  1. Click on the view site information icon (Left to the YRL) 

  2. Click on the Connection is Secure menu   

  3. Click on Certificate is Valid option 

  4. Go to the Details tab and click on Export button 

  5. Save the certificate with a name 

5. Apply the saved Certificate .

  1. Go to the saved Location and double click the certificate . 

  2. Click on Install Certificate  and choose Current User Click Next 

  3. Choose Automatically select the certificate store based on type of certificate option and choose next 

  4. Click Finish 

  5. You will get a popup saying import is done successfully  , Click Ok 

6. Now restart the eclipse and follow the steps to install ADT tools .

 

Steps to Save Certificate in Microsoft Edge Browser:

Once you are in the ADT tools link Page (Now you are in step 4)

  1. Click on the lock icon left to the URL 

  2. Click on the connection is secure menu 

  3. Click on the certificate button next to close button 

  4. Go to the Details tab and Click on Export button 

  5. Save your certificate 

  6. Now resume from step 5 

NB : This Blog is written solely from my experience , knowledge and my vision . If any misleading and wrong information is conveyed please correct me in the comments.

#Knowledge_sharing_is_caring

entry.title: Free SAP BTP - Create new Subaccount
entry.content:
This article will show the creation of a new Subaccount in your recent SAP BTP global.

In order to create a new Subaccount, you need to be at the global account level (green rectangle) and then open the Create menu, selecting "Subaccount" (yellow ellipse).

A new screen will open, asking for important information about the new Subaccount, pay attention to the Region.

The region will have a BIG influence on what you can do (for free) in your SAP BTP account, I recommend taking a look at this link below to find out what are free services available in the region.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/viewServices

In the spoiler there is a small branch in the subject, but necessary for future peace of mind.... feel free to sneak peek 

Spoiler

There is a service called "Landscape Portal" responsible for admin tasks at SAP BTP, like Hana hibernation, support users, etc... I found out the hard way that this service is only available in EUR (cf-eu10 or cf-eu11), after getting crazy looking the region I choose ( Azure US East).

My advice is this service will be used every day to start the Hana system (I will explain why in the next article), this switch takes only 3 clicks. For us developers who are concerned about the new development performance, LAG, and possible expansion, look for the region closest to you. I just created another Subaccount in eu10 and everything else close to me in USA/Texas.

After the SAP BTP process the creation of your new Subaccount, you can go into it and create the new services (remember to choose free ones).

Also, now is a good time to back to the main global account and take a look into the finance part (Cost and Usage).

Here you can check and fix potential cost associate to you account. We are trying to stay at free tier / no-cost.

If you missed the 1st article how to create your global account, you can navigate to it below or go to next article showing how to create / deploy your first service.

 

< Previous  Next >

entry.title: End-to-End Guide: Deploying a Fiori App – From BTP Destination Creation to Public Cloud Deployment
entry.content:
Overview

Deploying an application in the public cloud requires a structured approach involving multiple configurations and integrations. This guide provides a detailed, step-by-step process to ensure a smooth deployment. The key steps include setting up destination connections, creating communication systems, building the app in Business Application Studio (BAS), deploying it, and configuring it in ABAP. This document also highlights important screenshot points to help visualize each step.

Step 1: Set Up the Destination Connection

  1. Open BTP Cockpit and Login: Access the BTP cockpit and log in to your account.
  2. Navigate to Sub-Account: Select and go to the relevant sub-account.
  3. Go to Destinations: Navigate to the "Destinations" section and create a new destination.
  4. Configure Destination Settings:
    • URL: Copy the hostname URL from the Communication Systems app and paste it into the destination URL field. Make sure that to use the URL of the development client to build and deploy an application. If the URL of customizing client is used, then it will lead to issues while deploying application.
    • Authentication Type: Use SAMLAssertion.
    • Audience: Retrieve the OAuth 2.0 SAML2 Audience from the technical data section in the Communication Systems app.

       

    • AuthnContextClassRef: Set it to urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PreviousSession.
    • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
    • HTML5.Timeout: 60000
    • nameIdFormat: urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
    • sap-client: Specify your client (e.g., 100).
    • WebIDEEnabled: true
    • WebIDESystem: Specify your system (e.g., KOE).
    • WebIDEUsage: dev_abap, odata_abap, bsp_execute_abap, ui5_execute_abap.

       

  5. Download Trust File: Click on "Download Trust" to save the trust configuration locally.

Step 2: Create a Communication System in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Access the Communication Systems App: Open the app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

     

  2. Create a New Communication System:
    • Specify the system ID as BAS_<Subdomain Name> and provide a meaningful system name.

       

  3. Set Technical Data:
    • Go to the "Technical Data" section and select the checkbox for "Inbound Only".

       

    • Go to the identity provider and turn the SAML Bearer Assertion provider.

       

  4. Configure Identity Provider:
    • Enable the "SAML Bearer Assertion" provider.
    • Upload the trust file downloaded in the previous step.
    • After uploading the document fill the above SAML Bearer Issuer field by coping the address from the Signing certificate Subject from the “cfapps….” to till the last.

Step 3: Create the Application in BAS

  1. Open BAS: Access the SAP Business Application Studio.
  2. Create Application Using Template:
    • Select the "SAP Fiori Generator" template.
  3. Configure Application:
    • Select the template as "List Report Page" or required template.

       

    • Choose the data source, system, and OData service.

       

    • Select the main entity of the service.

       

  4. Add Project Attributes:
    • Provide details like project name, namespace, and deployment configurations.

       

       
  5. Add Deployment Configuration:
    • Add the required details for deployment.

       

  6. Configure Fiori Launchpad:
    • Add the required details for the launchpad configuration.

       

  7. Finalize and Create Project: Click "Next" and create the project.

Step 4: Deploy the App from BAS

  1. Open Integrated Terminal:
    • Right-click on the project folder and open the terminal.
  2. Run Deployment Command:
    • Execute "npm run deploy" in the terminal.

       

    • Verify details and confirm deployment by typing y. 
  3. Verify Deployment: Ensure the deployment completes successfully.

     

  4. After deploying the application, if you see the preview of the application, you will get a 404 error. That is because the deployed app should be configured and published in IAM apps in Eclipse.

Step 5: Configure the App in ABAP (Eclipse)

  1. Open Eclipse: Launch Eclipse and open the package used for deployment.
  2. Create IAM App:
    • Right-click on the package and create an IAM app.

       

    • Provide name, description, and set Application Type to "External App".
    • Assign a transport request (TR) and finish the setup.

       

  3. Add Fiori Launchpad App ID:
    • Locate the app ID under the package: Fiori User Interface > Launchpad App Descriptor Items.
    • Add this ID to the IAM app configuration.

       

  4. Create Business Catalog:
    • Create and assign the app to a new business catalog.

       

    • Click on next and select the TR and click on next.
    • Then the dialog opens for creating the Business catalog app assignment, set the name and description and select next and choose the TR.

       

  5. Publish the Catalog and App:
    • Publish the business catalog locally.
    • Publish the IAM app. 
  6. Check the preview of the application form the deployed link.

Step 6: Assign the App to Users

  1. Verify Published IAM App and Catalog:
    • Open the "Display IAM App" to check the published app.

       

    • Verify the catalog in the "Business Catalogs" app.

       

  2. Create Spaces and Pages:
    • Define spaces and pages in the Fiori launchpad.
    • Assign the app to a role and add users to the role.
  3. Verify User Access: Ensure the app is available on the user’s home screen.

Summary

This guide walks you through deploying an SAP Fiori app in the public cloud, covering destination setup, BAS development, deployment, and ABAP configuration. Step-by-step instructions with screenshot placeholders ensure clarity. Following this process minimizes errors for a smooth deployment.

entry.title: View Business Event Logs
entry.content:
This analytical application lets you review business events logged in Business Event Logging.

Screen Areas

Information filter

You can use the information filter at the top of the screen to select which data is displayed in the chart and table. This allows you to view your business event log data in various ways. You must enter the Object and Time Interval, as they are mandatory filters. Additionally, you can use other filters such as “Object Component,” “User,” and so on. The Producer Namespace helps you differentiate between SAP-provided and customer events. SAP-provided business events always carry the producer namespace “sap.s4.beh.”

Fig. 2: Filter bar

Visual filter

Visual filter provides a graphical interface for interacting with and filtering your data. It allows you to view and filter the frequency of events based on "Year and Calendar Week," as well as by "Object Component," such as Item or Schedule Line.

Fig. 3: Visual filter bar

Bar chart

The bar chart offers a concise overview of the frequency of specific events. By selecting a bar, you can filter the entries in the table. Additionally, utilize the View By filter to explore different dimensional data. You also have the option to change the chart type from the default bar chart to other types of charts.

Fig. 4: Bar chart in Hybrid view

Fig. 5: Bar chart in Chart-only view

The "Hybrid view," also referred to as "Chart and Table View," is the default setting that displays both the chart and the table simultaneously. Additionally, you have the option to toggle between "Chart-only view" and "Table-only view" within the chart area.

Fig. 6: Toolbar in chart area

Table

The table located at the bottom of the screen provides additional details regarding the logged business events, including Business Event Type, Object IDs, Performed By, and Producer Namespace. In the "Settings" button, you have the option to customize the displayed columns to include information such as Object Keys, Node ID, Source of Data, and more. Additionally, you can utilize the "Group By" and "Sort by" features for the columns.

Upon selecting a table entry, you will be directed to the details page of the business event. Selecting the Object IDs gives you an overview of all the events for an object (components), and the Performed By column provides access to the user contact card.

Fig. 7: Table in Table-only view

Details page

The specifics of the event are presented on the object page. This page provides a summary of the key properties associated with the event.

  • Event type and version
  • Object component and IDs
  • Date of execution
  • User
  • Version and release status of the event (mostly “released” and “deprecated”
  • Source system
  • Transaction codes, if available
  • Source of Data: currently only “Business Event” = externally released business events by SAP, or customer events with event binding

A raised event may include Event Qualifiers and Field Changes, which are presented in separate tables.

Event qualifiers are occasionally used in generic business events. Their values can offer more precise semantics to the business event.

Field Changes provides a comprehensive list of all value modifications recorded in the logged business events. For events with a deep structure, this table additionally displays the path to the changed field.

EventUUID serves as a distinct identifier for the event, while NodeID identifies a specific instance of an object component, such as a Sales Order Item. Additionally, the Transaction ID denotes the technical session(SAP LUW) during which the event was executed within the system.

Fig. 8: Details page

To enable logging of all business event data in the BEL logging configuration, check the All Data option for the SAP Object Type. You can access the logged business event data by clicking the "View Business Event Data" button on the details page.

Fig. 9: Business event data in a popup

Release Information

 

ProductAvailability
SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition2411 and later
SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition2021 FPS02* and later
SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise)2021 FPS02* and later
SAP BTP ABAP Environment2411

* In SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, the functionality of logging and viewing all business data is only available in 2023.

Fiori Apps Library Reference

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S34

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S29PCE

SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise): https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S29OP

entry.title: Unlock the expertise of top SAP professionals through live sessions - Register now!
entry.content:
With your SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can take advantage of expert-led live sessions, to dive deeper into focus topics, ask questions and maximize your skills


SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Basic Trial for Enterprise Automation with SAP
     March 20, 11:00 - 12:30 (CET)
  • Getting Started with the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
    March 27, 16:00 - 17:00 (CET)

Artificial Intelligence (AI)

  • SAP Business Technology Platform Pillars - AI Solutions
    March 14, 09:00 - 11:00 (CET) or from 16:00 - 18:00 (CET)
  • Managing the Full Lifecycle of AI Models on SAP Business Technology Platform
    March 31, 08:00 - 10:00 (CET) or from 16:00 - 18:00 (CET)

Enterprise Management

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales: Automatic Data Determination (Plant/Shipping/Route)
    March 19, 16:00 - 17:00 (CET)
  • Business Partner Master Data in SAP S/4HANA from the Purchasing Perspective
     March 20, 15:00 - 17:00 (CET)

As an SAP Learning Hub subscriber, you can join this live session to explore the various methods of data integration, with a focus on the best options for different scenarios. You will learn how to implement various data integration environments, ranging from a simple file import to an enterprise level data load. If you are involved in an incentive management implementation of any size, you don’t want to miss this session. Read more

Please note
You can find the full list of live sessions here and register directly with your SAP Learning Hub subscription.
